content.problemStatement:
Chef has a string and wants to transform it into a palindrome by inserting the minimum number of characters possible. A palindrome is a string that reads the same forwards and backwards. Chef can insert any character at any position in the string, and each insertion counts as one step. Help Chef determine the least number of insertions required to make the given string a palindrome.

Function DeclarationFunction NamecalculateMinInsertionscalculateMinInsertionscalculateMinInsertions - This function calculates the minimum number of insertions needed to make the given text a palindrome.

Parameters
inputTextinputTextinputText: The text for which the minimum insertions to form a palindrome are to be calculated.
The string consists of lowercase and/or uppercase English letters.
Return Value
Returns a number.
The returned value represents the minimum number of character insertions required to transform inputTextinputTextinputText into a palindrome.
The output is a non-negative integer indicating the count of insertions.
Constraints
1≤∣inputText∣≤50001 \leq |inputText| \leq 50001≤∣inputText∣≤5000
1≤T≤1001 \leq T \leq 1001≤T≤100
Sum of lengths of all strings across all test cases ≤105\leq 10^5≤105
inputTextinputTextinputText consists of uppercase and lowercase English letters.
Characters are case-sensitive ('A' and 'a' are treated as different characters).

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
Each of the next TTT lines contains a single string inputTextinputTextinputText representing Chef's text.


Output Format

For each test case, print a single line containing the minimum number of insertions Chef needs to make the given text a palindrome.
constraints: Sum of |S| over all test cases <= 100000
S contains only English letters and is case‑sensitive
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a non‑empty string S consisting of uppercase and/or lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the minimum number of character insertions required to transform S into a palindrome.

sampleTestCases.explanation: abc needs two insertions (e.g., add 'b' and 'c' to get abcba). racecar is already a palindrome, so 0. banana needs three insertions to become bananan.
abca → insert 'b' at the end → abcba (1). abcba is already a palindrome (0). abcd needs three insertions (e.g., dcbabcd). aaaa and xyzzyx are palindromes (0 each).
zzazz is a palindrome (0). mbadm needs two insertions to become mbdadbm (2). leetcode has LPS length 3, so 8‑3=5 insertions. a is a single character (0).

solution.approach: The minimum number of insertions needed to make a string a palindrome equals the length of the string minus the length of its longest palindromic subsequence (LPS). The LPS of a string S is the longest common subsequence (LCS) between S and its reverse Rev(S). We compute the LCS using a classic DP: dp[i][j] = length of LCS of the first i characters of S and the first j characters of Rev(S). Transition: if S[i‑1]==Rev[j‑1] then dp[i][j]=dp[i‑1][j‑1]+1 else dp[i][j]=max(dp[i‑1][j],dp[i][j‑1]). After filling the table, answer = n - dp[n][n]. The algorithm runs in O(n²) time and O(n²) memory per test case, which fits the given limits (total n ≤ 100 000).
solution.timeComplexity: O(n²) per test case, where n = |S|. Overall O(total length²) but bounded by the sum constraint (≤ 10⁵).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n²) integers for the DP table (≈ 25 MB for n=5000).

code.Python3:
import sys

def min_insertions(s: str) -> int:
    n = len(s)
    # use two rows to save memory
    prev = [0] * (n + 1)
    cur = [0] * (n + 1)
    rev = s[::-1]
    for i in range(1, n + 1):
        cur[0] = 0
        for j in range(1, n + 1):
            if s[i-1] == rev[j-1]:
                cur[j] = prev[j-1] + 1
            else:
                cur[j] = prev[j] if prev[j] > cur[j-1] else cur[j-1]
        prev, cur = cur, prev
    lps = prev[n]
    return n - lps

data = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T = int(data[0])
out = []
for i in range(1, T + 1):
    out.append(str(min_insertions(data[i].strip())))
print('\n'.join(out))
